Medicare for all is when everyone gets health care through Medicare, which is the program that takes care of older people and some sick people.
Single-payer healthcare is when one company or organization pays for all the doctors' visits, medicine, and other health care you need.
So, in Medicare for all, it's like if your parents paid for all your doctor visits and medicine, but only for older people and some sick people.
In single-payer healthcare, it's like if one big company, like a giant piggy bank, paid for all your doctor visits and medicine, no matter who you are or how old you are.
Both are about making sure everyone has the same chance to be healthy, but they do it a little differently.
